chapter_context.summary: The decline of King David's health sparks a succession crisis as Adonijah attempts to seize the throne; Nathan and Bathsheba intervene, leading to Solomon being anointed and proclaimed king.

outline.text: King David's Old Age and Abishag's Care
outline.explanation: This section focuses on King David's Old Age and Abishag's Care (1:1-4), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Adonijah Exalts Himself and Prepares to Reign
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Adonijah Exalts Himself and Prepares to Reign (1:5-10), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Nathan and Bathsheba Plot to Inform King David
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Nathan and Bathsheba Plot to Inform King David (1:11-27), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: David Directs Solomon's Anointing; Solomon Proclaimed King
outline.explanation: This section focuses on David Directs Solomon's Anointing; Solomon Proclaimed King (1:28-40), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Adonijah Hears the News; Flees to the Altar; Solomon Spares Him
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Adonijah Hears the News; Flees to the Altar; Solomon Spares Him (1:41-53), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

key_themes.title: Wisdom and Folly
key_themes.description: Solomon's wisdom is the gift of God, but his ultimate moral compromise reveals that knowledge without obedience leads to folly.

key_themes.title: The Temple and God's Dwelling
key_themes.description: The construction of the Temple represents a physical home for the Ark, though Solomon recognizes that even the highest heavens cannot contain God.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the events of Chapter 1, what spiritual choices are you prompted to make to remain faithful to the LORD?
How does this chapter show the difference between outward religious form and inward devotion?

cultural_historical.title: The Golden Age of Israel
cultural_historical.text: Under Solomon, Israel reached its economic and geographic zenith, dominating trade routes and exhibiting unparalleled wealth.
